name: Keystone Engine (Multi-Architecture Assembler)
pricing_notes: Open source (GPL-2.0); Nguyen Anh Quynh (Capstone/Unicorn author)

performance.notes: C library backend; very fast assembly

agent_readiness.notes: Multi-architecture assembler — complementary to Capstone (disassembler). from keystone import *. ks = Ks(KS_ARCH_X86, KS_MODE_64). encoding, count = ks.asm('mov rax, 1; syscall'). Architectures: x86/64, ARM/A64, MIPS, PowerPC, SPARC, HEXAGON. Assembles assembly text to machine code bytes. Pwntools uses keystone internally. CTF exploit development: assemble custom shellcode. Binary patching: generate replacement bytes for patches.

gotchas: Install can fail on some platforms — check binary wheels availability
AT&T vs Intel syntax variants must be specified explicitly
Some RISC architectures require alignment constraints in code
best_when: Assembling custom shellcode for exploit development
Binary patching: generate machine code for instruction replacement
CTF challenges requiring custom assembly generation
Complementing Capstone disassembly with assembly capability
avoid_when: When pwntools asm() covers the shellcode needs
summary: Keystone Engine is a multi-architecture assembler — the companion to Capstone (disassembler). Ks(KS_ARCH_X86, KS_MODE_64).asm('mov rax, 1; syscall') returns machine code bytes. Assembles x86/ARM/MIPS/SPARC/HEXAGON. Used in pwntools for shellcode generation. CTF exploit development: generate custom shellcode bytes. Binary patching: create replacement instructions. GPL-2.0.
